english: — ču-mdá (chu mda') a jet, a spouting forth of water Med. — ču-mdó (chu mdo) 'mouth (of a river), spout (of a tea-pot)' Sch.; but v. mču (mchu). — ču-mdóg (chu mdog) the colour of urine Med. — ču-rdó (chu rdo) C. small rounded pebbles, as in brooks. — ču-nág (chu nag) inundation, flood (?) Ma. — ču-rnág (chu rnag) matter, pus Sch. — ču-snód (chu snod) 1. pitcher, jug. 2. Schr.: chamber-pot (yet in W. at least this article of luxury is not known). — ču-pa (chu pa) water-carrier. — ču-pyág-pa (chu phyag pa) is enumerated among other synonyms to grú-pa (gru pa), signifying a ferry-man, water-man. — ču-prán (chu phran) a little river, brook. — ču-bár (chu bar) 1. ('between the waters') isthmus, neck of land. 2. p. n. of a place in Tibet. — ču-bál (chu bal) n. of an aquatic plant Wdn. — ču-bún (chu bun) white paint for the face Sch. — ču-búr (chu bur) 1. bubble, also čui (chu'i) ču-búr (chu bur) 2. blister, bladder, vesicle, e.g. occasioned by a burn or a vesicatory Lt. 3. boil, ulcer, abscess Thgy., 4. šai (sha'i) ču-búr (chu bur) a word describing the foetus five days after conception Thgy. — ču-bur-čan (chu bur can) 1. n. of a hell Thgy. 2. the eye Schr. — ču-bo (chu bo) river, frq., ču-bo-ri (chu bo ri) n. of a mountain with a monastery two days' journey from Lhasa Glr. — ču-byá (chu bya) a water-bird; Sch.: ču-byá (chu bya) dkár-po (dkar po) swan, ču-byá (chu bya) mgo-dmár (mgo dmar) stork (not known in W.). — ču-byí (chu byi) water-rat Sch. — ču-lbág (chu lbag) v. lbag (lbag). — ču-sbúr (chu sbur) 1. Sch.: 'driftwood and the like', prob. more correctly: thin pieces of wood, chips, chaff etc. floating on the water. 2. water-beetle Med. — ču-sbrúl (chu sbrul) water-snake, not a mythological conception, like klu (klu), but a really existing animal, though for Tibetans a somewhat faboulous one, as they have never seen the creature itself. The eel (Sch.) can hardly be meant by it. — ču-míg (chu mig) 1. spring, fountain frq. 2. n. of a vein Med. — ču-rtsá (chu rtsa) v. ču-ču (chu chu), as a separate article. — ču-tságs (chu tshags) 1. a strainer, sieve, 2. watering-pot. — ču-tsán (chu tshan) 1. hot water, 2. warm water, not too hot for drinking Med. 3. a hot spring Sch. — ču-tsód (chu tshod) 1. the clepsydra or water-clock of ancient India. 2. clock in general, ču-tsod-kór-lo (chu tshod 'khor lo) a wheel-clock. 3. the Indian hour = 1/60 Kyim (khyim) or 24 minutes. 4. the European hour; W.: *ču-tsód (chu tshod) nyis (nyis) ma (ma) leb* it is not yet two o'clock. — ču-°dzin (chu 'dzin) po (po). cloud Mil. — ču-rdzá (chu rdza) earthen vessel for water, water-jar. — ču-žéṅ (chu zheng) (Lex. परिणाह) long and broad, area, superficial extent, ču-žéṅ (chu zheng) kru-brgyád-pa (khru brgyad pa) eight cubits long and broad Dzl.; also ču-žeṅ-gáb-pa (chu zheng gab pa), e.g. ču-žeṅ-gáb-pa-la (chu zheng gab pa la) dpag-tsád (dpag tshad) brgyád-ču (brgyad bcu) Glr.; ču-žeṅ-srab-túg (chu zheng srab mthug) in length, breadth and height; also separately: čur (chur) dpag-tsád (dpag tshad) ༢༤༠༠, žeṅ-du (zheng du) yaṅ (yang) ༢༤༠༠, mta-skór-du (mtha' skor du) dpag-tsád (dpag tshad) ༡༠༠༠༠ yód-pa (yod pa) 2500 miles in length, 2500 miles in breadth, 10000 in circumference; yet except in this connexion ču (chu) alone is never used for length. As another signification of ču-žeṅ-gáb-pa (chu zheng gab pa) Schr. mentions moreover: proportioned, symmetrical; others have: beautiful, great, considerable, which e.g. is its proper meaning in: ču-žeṅ-gáb-pa (chu zheng gab pa) nya-grodha (nya gro dha) Stg. the stately fig-tree. — ču-zém (chu zem) water-tub. — ču-zlá (chu zla) 1. the image, the reflexion of the moon in the water; a sort of deception of the senses by witchcraft. 2. the water-month, the first month. — ču-gzár (chu gzar) a large ladle Cs. — ču-bzóm (chu bzom) a covered bucket for carrying water. — ču-óbs (chu 'obs) water-ditch Sch. — ču-yar (chu yar) col. water-rat (?) — ču-rágs (chu rags) dam, dike. — ču-ri (chu ri) 'hill of water', billow. — ču-rúd (chu rud) water rushing in, inundation, deluge. — ču-rlábs (chu rlabs) wave, billow Dzl. — ču-lág (chu lag) the arm of a river Glr. — ču-lúd (chu lud) Sch. dung, manure (?) — ču-ló (chu lo) n. of an edible plant S.g. — ču-lóg (chu log) floods. — ču-lón (chu lon), dam, dike Tar. 56, 15. Liš. — ču-šiṅ (chu shing) wood drifted away by the water = ču-grám-gyi (chu gram gyi) šiṅ (shing) v. above; or the translation of कदली plantain or banana-tree with its spongy wood, in the place of which the Tibetan thinks of the óm-bu ('om bu), a shrub of similar qualities, at any rate a symbol of perishableness, of the frailty of life. — ču-šúṅ (chu shung) Sch. surface of the water (?) — ču-šél (chu shel) v. šel (shel). — ču-šóṅ (chu shong) Dzl. ༧༢, ༡༧. a ravine containing water. — ču-sá (chu sa) river-mud, as manure. — ču-sér (chu ser) 1. animal water, serum, whether normal, or of a morbid character Med. — 2. matter, pus. — ču-sráṅ (chu srang), = 1/60 ču-tsod (chu tshod), i.e. a minute; the Indian or Tibetan minute is
